title: Extreme Environments: reading informational text; The Arctic Circle
description: 3rd grade reading comprehension practice using informational text
topic_path: Reading for understanding: informational text; The Arctic Circle
Extreme Environments: reading informational text; The Arctic Circle

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage, then answer the TWO practice questions.

___

##The Arctic Circle##

###Where in the World###

![A map of the Arctic Circle.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a6a40dfb03d20139e1c77dab9f76b13c.png)

![A map of the whole earth with the arctic circle marked with a red line.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/625195854cca80e5efcb8ede17f3aeaa.jpg)

Think about a globe. At the top, you'll find the Arctic Circle. This imaginary line around the earth is very far north. It includes parts of these countries and territories:

- Canada
- Finland
- Greenland
- Iceland
- Norway
- Russia
- Sweden
- USA (Alaska)

In the winter, the sun doesn't rise in parts of the Arctic Circle. This means there can be up to 24 hours of darkness. But in the summer, the opposite happens. The **midnight sun** rises and shines for 24 hours a day. Imagine that!

###Living in the Arctic Circle###

What’s it like to live in the Arctic Circle? Well, many people may find this extreme climate inhospitable. After all, the winters are long and very cold. Winter temperatures stay well below freezing all season long. The wind chill is so cold that it can freeze your skin in seconds. Blinding storms can make travel very difficult. People can and do live here, though. In fact, people have made the Arctic home for thousands of years. And today, there are many thriving communities that probably don’t seem too different from your own—well, apart from the weather, maybe! There are communities with shopping, movie theaters, schools, and more.

![An aerial view of Tromsø, a built-up city on the water.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/572243fbbc2503fde230bcd7e9a85956.jpg)   
*Tromsø is a thriving city in Norway, inside the Arctic Circle.* 

![Some small, brightly colored buildings near a snow-covered hill, and some dogs lying nearby.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5ab7ffa4fafb6eb1846fcaf9b62628ac.jpg)   
*Siorapaluk, Greenland is a smaller community in the Arctic Circle*

###Animals of the Arctic Circle ###

Many different animals make their homes in the Arctic. Unlike Antarctica, which is home to very few land animals, there are many that live in the Arctic. Polar bears, reindeer, rabbits, musk oxen, foxes, and lots of other animals make their home in the Arctic Circle. These animals are well-adapted to their environment. Many have thick fur or hair and feet that are good for walking on snow. The Arctic waters are also filled with life. Whales, seals, sharks, and many other kinds of marine creatures live in these icy, northern seas.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Questions##

This question has two parts. Answer Part A, then Part B.

###Part A##

Read this sentence from the passage.

“Well, many people may find this extreme climate __inhospitable__.” 

**What is the meaning of ​the word __inhospitable__?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

###Part B###

**Which sentence from the passage best helps in understanding the meaning of  __inhospitable__​?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

*Psst! Don't forget to choose an answer for both questions* :)

options.explanation:
Estimated 760L

*Image source: [“Arctic circle”](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Arctic_circle.png) is in the [public domain](https://creativecommons.org/share-your-work/public-domain/pdm/).*

*Image source: [“World map with arctic circle”](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:World_map_with_arctic_circle.jpg) by Cburnett is licensed under [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.en).*

*Image source: [“Image”](https://nsidc.org/sites/nsidc.org/files/images//Siorapaluk-greenland-with-dogs-lg.jpg) courtesy of the National Snow and Ice Data Center, University of Colorado, Boulder.*

*Image source: [“Tromsø sentrum (5835702754)”](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Troms%C3%B8_sentrum_(5835702754).jpg) by The Municipality of Tromsø is licensed under [CC BY 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/deed.en).*

choices.content: impossible to live in
friendly to live in
fun to live in
lonely to live in
“The wind chill is so cold that it can freeze your skin in seconds.”
“People can and do live here, though.”
“In fact, people have made the Arctic home for thousands of years.”
“There are communities with shopping, movie theaters, schools, and more.”

This question has two parts. Answer Part A, then Part B.

###Part A###

 Read this sentence from the passage.

“Well, many people may find this __extreme__ climate inhospitable.” 

**What is the meaning of ​the word __extreme__ as it is used in this sentence?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

###Part B###

**Which of these words is closest to the meaning of __extreme__​ as it is used in the sentence?*

[[☃ radio 2]]

*Image source: [“Tromsø sentrum (5835702754)”](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Troms%C3%B8_sentrum_(5835702754).jpg) by The Municipality of Tromsø is licensed under [CC BY 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/deed.en).*

choices.content: “The wind chill is so cold that it can freeze your skin in seconds.”
“In the winter, the sun doesn't rise in parts of the Arctic Circle.”
“In fact, people have made the Arctic home for thousands of years.”
“Many different animals make their homes in the Arctic.”
“There are communities with shopping, movie theaters, schools, and more.”

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage, then answer the practice question.

___

##The Arctic Circle##

###Where in the World###

![A map of the Arctic Circle.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a6a40dfb03d20139e1c77dab9f76b13c.png)

![A map of the whole earth with the arctic circle marked with a red line.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/625195854cca80e5efcb8ede17f3aeaa.jpg)

Think about a globe. At the top, you'll find the Arctic Circle. This imaginary line around the earth is very far north. It includes parts of these countries and territories:

- Canada
- Finland
- Greenland
- Iceland
- Norway
- Russia
- Sweden
- USA (Alaska)

In the winter, the sun doesn't rise in parts of the Arctic Circle. This means there can be up to 24 hours of darkness. But in the summer, the opposite happens. The **midnight sun** rises and shines for 24 hours a day. Imagine that!

###Living in the Arctic Circle###

What’s it like to live in the Arctic Circle? Well, many people may find this extreme climate inhospitable. After all, the winters are long and very cold. Winter temperatures stay well below freezing all season long. The wind chill is so cold that it can freeze your skin in seconds. Blinding storms can make travel very difficult. People can and do live here, though. In fact, people have made the Arctic home for thousands of years. And today, there are many thriving communities that probably don’t seem too different from your own—well, apart from the weather, maybe! There are communities with shopping, movie theaters, schools, and more.

![An aerial view of Tromsø, a built-up city on the water.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/572243fbbc2503fde230bcd7e9a85956.jpg)   
*Tromsø is a thriving city in Norway, inside the Arctic Circle.* 

![Some small, brightly colored buildings near a snow-covered hill, and some dogs lying nearby.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5ab7ffa4fafb6eb1846fcaf9b62628ac.jpg)   
*Siorapaluk, Greenland is a smaller community in the Arctic Circle*

###Animals of the Arctic Circle ###

Many different animals make their homes in the Arctic. Unlike Antarctica, which is home to very few land animals, there are many that live in the Arctic. Polar bears, reindeer, rabbits, musk oxen, foxes, and lots of other animals make their home in the Arctic Circle. These animals are well-adapted to their environment. Many have thick fur or hair and feet that are good for walking on snow. The Arctic waters are also filled with life. Whales, seals, sharks, and many other kinds of marine creatures live in these icy, northern seas.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Question##

**Pick TWO details from the passage that support the main idea that even though the Arctic has an extreme climate, living things can survive.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

*This text from the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) provides important information about Antarctica, an extremely cold region on Earth.*

##What is Antarctica?##

by NASA

![A penguin in the snow.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/307e3a6d3030db7b294cf4d29712e271.jpg)

Antarctica is a continent. It is Earth’s fifth largest continent. Antarctica is covered in ice. Antarctica covers Earth’s South Pole.

###What is Antarctica like?###

Antarctica is the coldest place on Earth. The temperature in the winter is cold enough to freeze water all the time. The temperature in the middle of Antarctica is much colder than the temperature on the [[☃ definition 1]]. 

Antarctica has two seasons: summer and winter. Earth is [[☃ definition 2]] in space and the direction of tilt never changes. During summer, Antarctica is on the side of Earth tilted toward the sun. It is always sunny. In winter, Antarctica is on the side of Earth away from the sun. Then, the continent is always dark.

Antarctica is a desert. It does not rain or snow a lot there. When it snows, the snow does not melt and builds up over many years to make large, thick sheets of ice, called ice sheets. Antarctica is made up of lots of ice in the form of glaciers, ice shelves, and icebergs.

Antarctica has no trees or bushes. The only plants that can live in a place that cold are moss and [[☃ definition 3]]. 

###Who lives in Antarctica?###

Antarctica is too cold for people to live there for a long time. Scientists take turns going there to study the ice. Tourists visit Antarctica in the summers. The oceans around Antarctica are home to many types of whales. Antarctica is also home to seals and penguins. 

[[☃ explanation 2]]

___

##Practice Question##

**Pick TWO ways the ideas in the passages are similar.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: the area where land meets the sea or ocean
to be at a slant or tipped to one side

a group of simple plants* that usually live in water and don't have leaves or roots, including seaweed and pond scum.   

*technically, "plant-like organisms" :)
